items.title: Confidence intervals
items.description: Two-sided tests at α and (1−α) confidence intervals give equivalent reject/fail-to-reject decisions; CIs also report effect size and uncertainty.

items.title: t and Normal distributions
items.description: Test statistics reference sampling distributions—especially Normal and Student's t—to convert observed values into p-values.

items.title: Precision, recall, and F1
items.description: Type I errors resemble false positives (flagging an effect that is not real); Type II errors resemble false negatives (missing a real effect). Fixed predictions use metric tradeoffs; hypothesis tests address whether metric differences exceed chance.

items.title: LLM-as-judge evaluation
items.description: Aggregate judge scores across prompts can be compared with t-tests or proportion tests, but judge bias and variance affect both Type I/II rates—design rubrics and sample sizes accordingly.
